parts.title: SKILL 7: LISTEN FOR NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS
parts.instruction: Negative expressions are very common in the short dialogues, and the most common kind of correct response to a negative statement is a positive statement containing a word with an opposite meaning.

questions.passage:
(man) Are you going to take out the trash?
(woman) I have no time to do it.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She is very busy.

reason.en: The woman states, 'I have no time to do it.' This is a negative expression that implies she is very busy and cannot perform the task.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menyatakan, 'Saya tidak punya waktu untuk melakukannya.' Ini adalah ekspresi negatif yang menyiratkan bahwa dia sangat sibuk dan tidak dapat melakukan tugas tersebut.

options.text: She has lots of free time.

reason.en: The woman explicitly states she has 'no time,' which is the opposite of having 'lots of free time.'
reason.id: Wanita tersebut secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa dia 'tidak punya waktu,' yang merupakan kebalikan dari memiliki 'banyak waktu luang.'

options.text: It is not necessary to take out the trash.

reason.en: The woman's statement is about her lack of time, not about the necessity of taking out the trash. The dialogue does not provide information about whether it is necessary.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita tersebut adalah tentang kurangnya waktu, bukan tentang keharusan membuang sampah. Dialog tidak memberikan informasi tentang apakah itu perlu.

options.text: She will do it if she has time.

reason.en: The woman says she has 'no time,' which means she cannot do it now. The statement does not suggest she will do it later if she has time; it only indicates her current inability.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut mengatakan bahwa dia 'tidak punya waktu,' yang berarti dia tidak bisa melakukannya sekarang. Pernyataan tersebut tidak menyarankan bahwa dia akan melakukannya nanti jika punya waktu; itu hanya menunjukkan ketidakmampuannya saat ini.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(woman) Are you worried about the interview?
(man) It's unimportant to me.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The interview is very important.

reason.en: The man states the interview is 'unimportant to me,' which is the opposite of important. Therefore, this answer is incorrect.
reason.id: Pria itu menyatakan bahwa wawancara itu 'tidak penting bagiku', yang merupakan kebalikan dari penting. Oleh karena itu, jawaban ini salah.

options.text: He is worried about the interview.

reason.en: The man explicitly states the interview is 'unimportant to me,' directly contradicting the idea that he is worried.
reason.id: Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa wawancara itu 'tidak penting bagiku', yang secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa dia khawatir.

options.text: What he's wearing to the interview is important.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention anything about what the man is wearing to the interview. This is an irrelevant detail.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak menyebutkan apa pun tentang apa yang dikenakan pria itu untuk wawancara. Ini adalah detail yang tidak relevan.

options.text: He is not concerned about the interview.

reason.en: The man's statement 'It's unimportant to me' directly means he is not concerned about the interview. This aligns with Skill 7, where a negative statement often implies the opposite meaning.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu 'tidak penting bagiku' secara langsung berarti dia tidak peduli dengan wawancara tersebut. Ini sejalan dengan Skill 7, di mana pernyataan negatif sering kali menyiratkan makna sebaliknya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you have all the notes for Psychol ogy 101?
(man) I didn't miss a single lecture.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: He has almost all the notes.

reason.en: The man states he didn't miss a single lecture, which means he attended all of them. Option [0] suggests he 'almost' has all the notes, implying he missed some, contradicting his statement.
reason.id: Pria itu menyatakan bahwa dia tidak melewatkan satu kuliah pun, yang berarti dia menghadiri semuanya. Pilihan [0] menyarankan bahwa dia 'hampir' memiliki semua catatan, menyiratkan bahwa dia melewatkan beberapa, yang bertentangan dengan pernyataannya.

options.text: His attendance was perfect.

reason.en: The phrase 'I didn't miss a single lecture' is a double negative construction that emphasizes perfect attendance. Therefore, the man means his attendance was perfect.
reason.id: Frasa 'I didn't miss a single lecture' adalah konstruksi negatif ganda yang menekankan kehadiran yang sempurna. Oleh karena itu, pria itu berarti kehadirannya sempurna.

options.text: He went to all the lectures but one.

reason.en: The man explicitly states he did not miss 'a single lecture,' meaning zero lectures were missed. Option [2] suggests he missed one lecture, which is the opposite of what he said.
reason.id: Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa dia tidak melewatkan 'satu kuliah pun', yang berarti nol kuliah yang terlewat. Pilihan [2] menyarankan bahwa dia melewatkan satu kuliah, yang berlawanan dengan apa yang dia katakan.

options.text: He missed more than one psychology class.

reason.en: The man's statement 'I didn't miss a single lecture' directly contradicts the idea that he missed more than one class. This option is the opposite of the intended meaning.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu 'I didn't miss a single lecture' secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa dia melewatkan lebih dari satu kelas. Pilihan ini adalah kebalikan dari makna yang dimaksud.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you know the library's summer hours? I need to go there this evening.
(man) It's never open past six o'clock in the summer.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: They passed the library at 6:00.

reason.en: The dialogue states the library is never open past six, implying it closes at six, not that they passed it at six.
reason.id: Dialog menyatakan perpustakaan tidak pernah buka melewati jam enam, menyiratkan bahwa perpustakaan tutup jam enam, bukan bahwa mereka melewatinya jam enam.

options.text: The library opens at 6:00 in the summer.

reason.en: The man says it's 'never open past six,' which means it closes at six, not that it opens at six.
reason.id: Pria itu berkata 'tidak pernah buka melewati jam enam', yang berarti perpustakaan tutup jam enam, bukan buka jam enam.

options.text: The library closes at 6:00.

reason.en: The statement 'It's never open past six o'clock in the summer' directly implies that the library closes at 6:00 PM.
reason.id: Pernyataan 'Perpustakaan tidak pernah buka melewati jam enam di musim panas' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa perpustakaan tutup pada pukul 18:00.

options.text: You can't check out more than six books in the summer.

reason.en: The dialogue is about library hours, not the number of books that can be checked out.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut membahas jam operasional perpustakaan, bukan jumlah buku yang dapat dipinjam.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(woman) How often should I water the plants while you're gone?
(man) No more than once a week.
questions.question: 5. WHAT SHOULD THE WOMAN DO?

options.text: Water the plants once a day.

reason.en: The man explicitly states 'No more than once a week,' which contradicts watering the plants once a day.
reason.id: Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan 'Tidak lebih dari seminggu sekali,' yang bertentangan dengan menyiram tanaman setiap hari.

options.text: Give the plants no more water.

reason.en: The man says 'No more than once a week,' implying that some watering is allowed, not that the plants should receive no more water.
reason.id: Pria itu berkata 'Tidak lebih dari seminggu sekali,' menyiratkan bahwa penyiraman diizinkan, bukan bahwa tanaman tidak boleh disiram lagi.

options.text: Water the plants often while the man is gone.

reason.en: The man's instruction 'No more than once a week' is a restriction, not an encouragement to water often.
reason.id: Instruksi pria itu 'Tidak lebih dari seminggu sekali' adalah batasan, bukan dorongan untuk menyiram terlalu sering.

options.text: Give the plants a limited amount of water.

reason.en: The phrase 'No more than once a week' is a negative expression that limits the frequency of watering, meaning the woman should give the plants a limited amount of water.
reason.id: Frasa 'Tidak lebih dari seminggu sekali' adalah ekspresi negatif yang membatasi frekuensi penyiraman, yang berarti wanita itu harus memberikan jumlah air yang terbatas pada tanaman.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(man) Did you enjoy your meal? That restau rant is very famous.
(woman) The food was good, but I was dissatis fied with the service.
questions.question: 6. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: The service satisfied her.

reason.en: The woman explicitly states she was 'dissatisfied' with the service, which is the opposite of satisfied.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa dia 'tidak puas' dengan layanan tersebut, yang merupakan kebalikan dari puas.

options.text: The food was worse than the service.

reason.en: The woman states the food was 'good,' indicating it was not worse than the service. She was dissatisfied with the service, not that it was worse than the food.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menyatakan bahwa makanannya 'enak', yang menunjukkan bahwa itu tidak lebih buruk dari layanan. Dia tidak puas dengan layanan, bukan berarti itu lebih buruk dari makanannya.

options.text: She thought the service was bad.

reason.en: The woman uses the word 'dissatisfied,' which directly means she thought the service was bad or not good enough.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menggunakan kata 'tidak puas', yang secara langsung berarti dia menganggap layanan tersebut buruk atau tidak cukup baik.

options.text: Neither the food nor the service was satisfying.

reason.en: The woman states that the 'food was good,' contradicting the idea that neither the food nor the service was satisfying.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menyatakan bahwa 'makanannya enak', yang bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa baik makanan maupun layanan tidak memuaskan.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you think that Bob really wanted me to go home?
(man) He wasn't kidding when he told you to leave.
questions.question: 7. WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT BOB?

options.text: He told his kids to leave.

reason.en: The man states that Bob told 'you' (the woman) to leave, not his kids. This option changes the object of Bob's statement.
reason.id: Pria itu menyatakan bahwa Bob menyuruh 'kamu' (wanita itu) pergi, bukan anak-anaknya. Opsi ini mengubah objek dari pernyataan Bob.

options.text: He seriously wanted the woman to go.

reason.en: The man's statement 'He wasn't kidding when he told you to leave' directly implies that Bob was serious and genuinely wanted the woman to leave. 'Wasn't kidding' is a negative expression that means 'was serious'.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria itu 'He wasn't kidding when he told you to leave' secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa Bob serius dan benar-benar ingin wanita itu pergi. 'Wasn't kidding' adalah ekspresi negatif yang berarti 'serius'.

options.text: He was joking when he told the woman to leave.

reason.en: The man explicitly states 'He wasn't kidding,' which means the opposite of joking. This option directly contradicts the man's statement.
reason.id: Pria itu secara eksplisit menyatakan 'He wasn't kidding,' yang berarti kebalikan dari bercanda. Opsi ini secara langsung bertentangan dengan pernyataan pria itu.

options.text: He left with the woman.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention whether Bob left with the woman. The focus is on Bob's desire for the woman to leave.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak menyebutkan apakah Bob pergi bersama wanita itu. Fokusnya adalah pada keinginan Bob agar wanita itu pergi.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(woman) Can we finish this project before clos ing time?
(man) If we work on nothing else.
questions.question: 8. WHAT DOES THE MAN MEAN?

options.text: The project will take all their effort.

reason.en: The man's statement 'If we work on nothing else' implies that the project will require all of their attention and effort, meaning it will take all their effort to finish. This aligns with the meaning of 'nothing else' in this context.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria 'If we work on nothing else' menyiratkan bahwa proyek tersebut akan membutuhkan seluruh perhatian dan upaya mereka, yang berarti proyek tersebut akan menyita seluruh upaya mereka untuk menyelesaikannya. Hal ini sejalan dengan makna 'nothing else' dalam konteks ini.

options.text: They have no other work to do.

reason.en: The man's statement implies that they *must* work on nothing else to finish the project, not that they *have* no other work. The focus is on the project's demands, not their workload.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria menyiratkan bahwa mereka *harus* tidak mengerjakan hal lain selain proyek tersebut untuk menyelesaikannya, bukan bahwa mereka *tidak memiliki* pekerjaan lain. Fokusnya adalah pada tuntutan proyek, bukan beban kerja mereka.

options.text: It's impossible to finish.

reason.en: The man's statement suggests that finishing the project is possible *if* they dedicate all their effort to it, not that it's impossible. The condition 'If we work on nothing else' indicates a possibility.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria menunjukkan bahwa menyelesaikan proyek itu mungkin *jika* mereka mencurahkan seluruh upaya mereka untuk itu, bukan bahwa itu tidak mungkin. Kondisi 'If we work on nothing else' menunjukkan sebuah kemungkinan.

options.text: They aren't even close to finishing the project.

reason.en: The man's statement implies that the project will take *all* their effort, which is a significant amount, but it doesn't necessarily mean they are 'not even close' to finishing. It focuses on the effort required, not the progress made.
reason.id: Pernyataan pria menyiratkan bahwa proyek tersebut akan membutuhkan *seluruh* upaya mereka, yang merupakan jumlah yang signifikan, tetapi itu tidak berarti mereka 'bahkan belum dekat' untuk menyelesaikannya. Pernyataan itu berfokus pada upaya yang diperlukan, bukan kemajuan yang telah dicapai.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(man) Would you mind staying an hour more?
(woman) I'd rather not.
questions.question: 9. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT STAYING?

options.text: She doesn't mind an hour more.

reason.en: The woman explicitly states 'I'd rather not,' which is a negative response, contradicting this option.
reason.id: Wanita secara eksplisit menyatakan 'I'd rather not,' yang merupakan respons negatif, bertentangan dengan pilihan ini.

options.text: She'd rather stay more than an hour.

reason.en: The woman says 'I'd rather not,' indicating she does not want to stay an hour more, not that she'd prefer to stay longer than an hour.
reason.id: Wanita mengatakan 'I'd rather not,' yang menunjukkan dia tidak ingin tinggal satu jam lagi, bukan bahwa dia lebih suka tinggal lebih dari satu jam.

options.text: It's better to stay than go.

reason.en: The woman's response 'I'd rather not' clearly indicates she does not prefer to stay, making this option incorrect.
reason.id: Respons wanita 'I'd rather not' dengan jelas menunjukkan bahwa dia tidak lebih suka tinggal, membuat pilihan ini salah.

options.text: She prefers to leave.

reason.en: The phrase 'I'd rather not' is a polite way of saying 'I prefer not to,' directly answering the man's request to stay longer. This implies she prefers to leave.
reason.id: Frasa 'I'd rather not' adalah cara sopan untuk mengatakan 'Saya lebih suka tidak,' yang secara langsung menjawab permintaan pria untuk tinggal lebih lama. Ini menyiratkan bahwa dia lebih suka pergi.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(woman) Would you recommend the hotel where you stayed in New York?
(man) The hotel provided service that was unequaled!
questions.question: 10. WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT THE HOTEL?

options.text: The service at the hotel wasn't too good.

reason.en: The man says the service was 'unequaled,' which means it was extremely good, not bad. This option states the opposite.
reason.id: Pria itu mengatakan bahwa layanan 'tak tertandingi', yang berarti sangat baik, bukan buruk. Pilihan ini menyatakan sebaliknya.

options.text: This hotel gave excellent service.

reason.en: The man uses the word 'unequaled' to describe the service, meaning it was exceptionally good and could not be matched by any other. This aligns with the meaning of 'excellent service'.
reason.id: Pria itu menggunakan kata 'tak tertandingi' untuk menggambarkan layanan, yang berarti layanan itu luar biasa baik dan tidak dapat ditandingi oleh yang lain. Ini sejalan dengan arti 'layanan yang sangat baik'.

options.text: The service at the hotel could have been improved.

reason.en: The word 'unequaled' indicates the highest level of service, not service that could be improved. This option suggests a deficiency that is contradicted by the man's statement.
reason.id: Kata 'tak tertandingi' menunjukkan tingkat layanan tertinggi, bukan layanan yang bisa ditingkatkan. Pilihan ini menyarankan kekurangan yang dibantah oleh pernyataan pria itu.

options.text: This hotel's service was the same as the service at other hotels.

reason.en: The word 'unequaled' means that the service was unique and could not be compared to others because it was so good. This option suggests a comparison that is negated by the man's strong positive statement.
reason.id: Kata 'tak tertandingi' berarti layanan itu unik dan tidak dapat dibandingkan dengan yang lain karena sangat baik. Pilihan ini menyarankan perbandingan yang dinegasikan oleh pernyataan positif yang kuat dari pria itu.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

parts.title: SKILL 8: LISTEN FOR DOUBLE NEGATIVE EXPRESSIONS

questions.passage:
(man) Do you think Ron Rogers will be elected?
(woman) Well, it's not completely impossible.
questions.question: 1. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN IMPLY ABOUT RON?

options.text: He'll definitely be elected.

reason.en: The woman's statement 'it's not completely impossible' is a double negative that implies uncertainty, not a definite outcome. This option states he will definitely be elected, which is the opposite of the implication.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita 'it's not completely impossible' adalah negasi ganda yang menyiratkan ketidakpastian, bukan hasil yang pasti. Pilihan ini menyatakan dia pasti akan terpilih, yang berlawanan dengan implikasinya.

options.text: The election is now complete.

reason.en: The dialogue is about whether Ron Rogers *will be* elected, indicating the election has not yet happened. This option states the election is complete, which is irrelevant to the implication.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut adalah tentang apakah Ron Rogers *akan* terpilih, yang menunjukkan bahwa pemilihan belum terjadi. Pilihan ini menyatakan pemilihan sudah selesai, yang tidak relevan dengan implikasinya.

options.text: She has high hopes for his chances.

reason.en: The phrase 'not completely impossible' suggests a possibility, but not necessarily high hopes. High hopes would imply a stronger belief in his success than this phrase conveys.
reason.id: Frasa 'not completely impossible' menunjukkan kemungkinan, tetapi belum tentu harapan yang tinggi. Harapan yang tinggi akan menyiratkan keyakinan yang lebih kuat pada keberhasilannya daripada yang disampaikan oleh frasa ini.

options.text: It may happen.

reason.en: The expression 'not completely impossible' is an 'almost negative' expression that implies that something is possible, though not guaranteed. This aligns with the idea that it 'may happen'.
reason.id: Ekspresi 'not completely impossible' adalah ekspresi 'hampir negatif' yang menyiratkan bahwa sesuatu itu mungkin, meskipun tidak dijamin. Ini sejalan dengan gagasan bahwa hal itu 'mungkin terjadi'.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 9: Listen for "Almost Negative" Expressions

questions.passage:
(woman) How was your tennis match today?
(man) I didn't serve well, and I didn't volley well either.
questions.question: 2. WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT HIS TENNIS GAME?

options.text: Both parts of his game were bad.

reason.en: The man states that he 'didn't serve well' and 'didn't volley well either.' The word 'either' indicates that both actions were negative. Therefore, both parts of his game were bad.
reason.id: Pria tersebut menyatakan bahwa dia 'tidak melakukan servis dengan baik' dan 'juga tidak melakukan voli dengan baik'. Kata 'either' menunjukkan bahwa kedua tindakan tersebut negatif. Oleh karena itu, kedua bagian permainannya buruk.

options.text: He served better than he volleyed.

reason.en: The man states that he 'didn't serve well' and 'didn't volley well either.' This implies both were bad, not that one was better than the other.
reason.id: Pria tersebut menyatakan bahwa dia 'tidak melakukan servis dengan baik' dan 'juga tidak melakukan voli dengan baik'. Ini menyiratkan keduanya buruk, bukan bahwa satu lebih baik dari yang lain.

options.text: Some parts of his game were better than others.

reason.en: The man states that he 'didn't serve well' and 'didn't volley well either.' This indicates that both aspects of his game were poor, not that some parts were better than others.
reason.id: Pria tersebut menyatakan bahwa dia 'tidak melakukan servis dengan baik' dan 'juga tidak melakukan voli dengan baik'. Ini menunjukkan bahwa kedua aspek permainannya buruk, bukan bahwa beberapa bagian lebih baik dari yang lain.

options.text: He played rather well.

reason.en: The man explicitly states that he 'didn't serve well' and 'didn't volley well either,' which is the opposite of playing well.
reason.id: Pria tersebut secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa dia 'tidak melakukan servis dengan baik' dan 'juga tidak melakukan voli dengan baik', yang merupakan kebalikan dari bermain baik.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(man) Was Gary prepared for the debate?
(woman) It is no surprise that he was unpre pared.
questions.question: 3. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN SAY ABOUT GARY?

options.text: It is a surprise that he was prepared.

reason.en: The woman states 'It is no surprise that he was unprepared.' This directly contradicts option 0, which claims it IS a surprise that he was prepared.
reason.id: Wanita menyatakan 'Tidak mengejutkan bahwa dia tidak siap.' Ini secara langsung bertentangan dengan opsi 0, yang mengklaim bahwa itu ADALAH kejutan bahwa dia siap.

options.text: He was not ready, as usual.

reason.en: The woman says 'It is no surprise that he was unprepared.' This implies that Gary is often unprepared, making option 1 ('He was not ready, as usual.') a correct inference.
reason.id: Wanita berkata 'Tidak mengejutkan bahwa dia tidak siap.' Ini menyiratkan bahwa Gary sering tidak siap, menjadikan opsi 1 ('Dia tidak siap, seperti biasa.') kesimpulan yang benar.

options.text: He prepared a really big surprise.

reason.en: The woman's statement is about Gary's lack of preparation for a debate, not about him preparing a surprise.
reason.id: Pernyataan wanita adalah tentang kurangnya persiapan Gary untuk debat, bukan tentang dia menyiapkan kejutan.

options.text: His strong preparation came as no surprise.

reason.en: The woman explicitly states that Gary was 'unprepared,' which is the opposite of strong preparation. The phrase 'no surprise' refers to his lack of preparation, not the presence of it.
reason.id: Wanita secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa Gary 'tidak siap,' yang merupakan kebalikan dari persiapan yang kuat. Frasa 'tidak mengejutkan' mengacu pada kurangnya persiapannya, bukan kehadirannya.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(man) Did you go out dancing with everyone else last night?
(woman) I was not feeling well, so I didn't go out.
questions.question: 4. WHAT DOES THE WOMAN MEAN?

options.text: She felt good enough to go out.

reason.en: The woman states 'I was not feeling well, so I didn't go out.' This directly contradicts the idea that she felt good enough to go out.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut menyatakan 'Saya tidak enak badan, jadi saya tidak keluar.' Ini secara langsung bertentangan dengan gagasan bahwa dia merasa cukup baik untuk keluar.

options.text: She went out to get some medicine.

reason.en: The dialogue does not mention anything about getting medicine. The reason given for not going out is feeling unwell.
reason.id: Dialog tersebut tidak menyebutkan apa pun tentang mendapatkan obat. Alasan yang diberikan untuk tidak keluar adalah merasa tidak enak badan.

options.text: She felt like dancing, so she went out with everyone.

reason.en: The woman explicitly states she 'didn't go out,' which is the opposite of going out with everyone.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa dia 'tidak keluar,' yang merupakan kebalikan dari pergi keluar bersama semua orang.

options.text: She stayed home because she was sick.

reason.en: The woman says 'I was not feeling well, so I didn't go out.' This directly implies that she stayed home because she was sick.
reason.id: Wanita tersebut mengatakan 'Saya tidak enak badan, jadi saya tidak keluar.' Ini secara langsung menyiratkan bahwa dia tinggal di rumah karena dia sakit.

questions.tested_skill: Listening Part A - Skill 7: Listen for Negative Expressions

questions.passage:
(woman) Do you think Paula understands what she's done?
(man) She isn't unaware of the trouble she's caused.
questions.question: 5. WHAT DOES THE MAN SAY ABOUT PAULA?
